video:video.video:title: South Florida wildfires burn thousands of acres in the Everglades
video:video.video:description: A pair of South Florida wildfires that torched thousands of acres in the Everglades over the weekend spread Monday as fire crews worked to contain them.

video:video.video:title: Why is everything so expensive? Business basics: Inflation
video:video.video:description: When the cost of nearly everything is rising, economists call it inflation. Even if paychecks also rise, inflation typically leads people feel like it's harder to make ends meet, and it often becomes a major political issue

video:video.video:title: Why is everything so expensive? Business basics: Inflation
video:video.video:description: When the cost of nearly everything is rising, economists call it inflation. Even if paychecks also rise, inflation typically leads people feel like it's harder to make ends meet, and it often becomes a major political issue

video:video.video:title: Invasive plant threatens livelihoods in Colombia’s largest coastal wetland
video:video.video:description: An invasive aquatic plant has spread rapidly across Colombia’s Cienaga Grande de Santa Marta over the past year.

video:video.video:title: Over 100 peacocks invade seaside town in northern Italy
video:video.video:description: The birds have reportedly been there for years, a colony living in an abandoned aeronautical complex.

video:video.video:title: Demi Moore: AI can never replace true art
video:video.video:description: Cannes Film Festival jury member Demi Moore says AI "isn't anything to fear" as it can never replace what "true art comes from." (May 12)

video:video.video:title: Demi Moore: AI can never replace true art
video:video.video:description: Cannes Film Festival jury member Demi Moore gives her thoughts on the rise of AI in films.

video:video.video:title: Cannes jury arrive for opening ceremony
video:video.video:description: Demi Moore, Stellan Skarsgård and Chloé Zhao arrive with jury head Park Chan-wook at the Cannes Film Festival, which is kicking off on the French Riviera. (May 12)

video:video.video:title: Researchers find rare and lost copy of oldest English known poem in Rome library
video:video.video:description: A new copy of the first poem ever to be transcribed in Old English has been unearthed in a library in Rome.

video:video.video:title: Trump departs Washington for high-stakes summit with Chinese President Xi Jinping
video:video.video:description: President Donald Trump departed Washington for Beijing Tuesday and his high-stakes summit with Chinese President Xi Jinping.

video:video.video:title: Cannes Film Festival opens with Moore, Jackson, Fonda on red carpet
video:video.video:description: The 2026 Cannes Film Festival kicked off with a red carpet featuring Demi Moore, Stellan Skarsgård, Jane Fonda, Tyrese Gibson and director Peter Jackson, who was given an honorary Palme d’Or at the opening ceremony.

video:video.video:title: Kuwait alleges that Iran attacked an island hosting a China-funded port before Trump's Beijing trip
video:video.video:description: Iran didn't immediately acknowledge the allegation by Kuwait, which came under repeated attack by Iran in the war and even during the shaky ceasefire still holding in the region.

video:video.video:title: Small study hints CAR-T cell therapy might help fight HIV
video:video.video:description: Scientists are supercharging patients' own immune cells to try to fight HIV without today's drugs. A small study is giving a hint that CAR-T cell therapy, already used for some cancers, might eventually work for HIV.

video:video.video:title: WHO chief thanks Spain for hantavirus ship evacuation efforts
video:video.video:description: The World Health Organization Director-General on Monday thanked Spanish authorities for safely evacuating passengers from a cruise ship hit by a deadly hantavirus outbreak.

video:video.video:title: Ship operator and employee are charged in crash that caused the deadly collapse of Baltimore bridge
video:video.video:description: Prosecutors have filed criminal charges against the operator of the ship that crashed into Baltimore’s Francis Scott Key Bridge leading to the deaths of six construction workers in March 2024.

video:video.video:title: Red carpet rolls out for 79th Cannes Film Festival
video:video.video:description: The red carpet is out, ready to welcome stars to the 79th Cannes Film Festival which kicks off today.

video:video.video:title: AP Top Stories May 12
video:video.video:description: Here’s the latest for Tuesday, May 12th: Trump rejects Iran’s latest proposal to end war; Trump proposes suspending federal gas tax; Alabama could eliminate largely Black Congressional district; White House Correspondents Dinner suspect pleads not guilty.

video:video.video:title: Cannes Film Festival rolls out iconic red carpet
video:video.video:description: The red carpet of the famous steps of Palais des Festivals is put in place, ready to welcome stars to the 79th Cannes Film Festival, in the South of France. (May 12)

video:video.video:title: Authorities say man struck and killed by plane at Denver airport intended to take his own life
video:video.video:description: The collision involving the Frontier Airlines plane sparked an engine fire that forced passengers to evacuate.

video:video.video:title: 'Good things are gonna happen:' Trump downplays differences over Iran as he heads to China summit
video:video.video:description: Trump said trade will be the focus, not the war, and predicted “good things” to come from the trip.

video:video.video:title: Death penalty opponents rally ahead of possible 600th Texas execution
video:video.video:description: Opponents of the death penalty gathered outside the Texas Capitol on Tuesday to urge Gov. Greg Abbott to halt the scheduled execution of Edward Busby, which would mark the 600th execution carried out in Texas since 1982.

video:video.video:title: Cannes 2026: Top moments from opening day
video:video.video:description: The 79th Cannes Film Festival launched on Tuesday, marking the start of 12 days of nonstop premieres that will culminate May 23 with the presentation of the prestigious Palme d’Or.

video:video.video:title: How does it feel to be on the jury at Cannes?
video:video.video:description: "The Substance" star Demi Moore, "Hamnet" director Chloé Zhao and "Sentimental Value" actor Stellan Skarsgård describe their reaction to being invited onto the Cannes Film Festival jury, led by South Korean director Park Chan-wook.

video:video.video:title: Trump to undergo medical checkup as scrutiny over his health continues
video:video.video:description: President Donald Trump is scheduled to undergo a medical and dental checkup later this month, as the White House says the president continues routine preventive care ahead of turning 80 in June.

video:video.video:title: How it feels to be on the jury at Cannes
video:video.video:description: Demi Moore, Chloé Zhao and Stellan Skarsgård describe being invited onto the Cannes Film Festival jury, led by director Park Chan-wook.

video:video.video:title: Ridglan Beagles find fans in pop star Debbie Gibson and Black Sabbath bass player
video:video.video:description: Pop star Debbie Gibson and Terry “Geezer” Butler, co-founder of the heavy metal band Black Sabbath, praised ongoing efforts to find new homes for roughly 1,500 beagles purchased from dog breeder and research facility Ridglan Farms outside of Madison.

video:video.video:title: AP Top Stories May 12
video:video.video:description: Here's the latest for Tuesday, May 12th: Trump departs for high-stakes China summit; Hegseth defends $1.5 trillion budget as Iran war cost climbs; Putin hails Russia's test-firing of new ballistic missile; Dutch hospital staff quarantines due to hantavirus patient.

video:video.video:title: Witness describes scene after man wildly shoots a rifle at drivers near Boston
video:video.video:description: A witness whose apartment overlooks the scene initially thought he was hearing construction equipment when the banging noises started.

video:video.video:title: Higher costs force Mississippi farmer to raise prices
video:video.video:description: Foot Print Farms CEO Cindy Ayers-Elliott tries to keep her produce affordable, but she has been forced to raise prices as she struggles to make a profit amid higher costs for gas, fertilizer and other essential items.

video:video.video:title: Trump: 'I don't think about Americans' financial' woes during Iran talks
video:video.video:description: President Donald Trump says he does not consider Americans’ financial strain “even a little bit” as he works to end the Iran conflict, comments that came as new data showed another sharp rise in consumer prices, driven in part by higher gas costs tied to the war.

video:video.video:title: 'I want that kind of jail to close'; French woman recalls her experience of US ICE detention centre
video:video.video:description: Marie-Therese Ross spoke to The Associated Press on Monday about her 16 harrowing days in federal immigration custody after being arrested on April 1 in Alabama following an alleged visa overstay, and the remarkable late-in-life love story that brought her to the U.S.

video:video.video:title: Trump says Marty Makary is out as Food and Drug Administration head
video:video.video:description: The head of the Food and Drug Administration, Dr. Marty Makary, is resigning after a rocky tenure that drew months of complaints from health industry executives, anti-abortion activists, vaping lobbyists and other allies of President Donald Trump.

video:video.video:title: Putin hails successful test launch of new intercontinental ballistic missile
video:video.video:description: Russian President Vladimir Putin hailed a successful test launch of a new intercontinental ballistic missile as a key part of efforts to modernize the country’s nuclear forces.

video:video.video:title: Musk, Cook and other prominent US executives invited to join Trump on trip to China
video:video.video:description: Prominent U.S. executives from Big Tech to agriculture have been invited to join President Donald Trump on his trip to China this week, according to a White House official.

video:video.video:title: Experts to study sites visited by hantavirus-infected couple in South America's Tierra del Fuego
video:video.video:description: Argentina’s Health Ministry says that a team of scientific experts investigating the origin of the hantavirus outbreak in Argentina will leave for the country’s southernmost city of Ushuaia at the end of this week or beginning of next.

video:video.video:title: Hegseth gets bipartisan grilling on rising costs of the Iran war and Trump's end game
video:video.video:description: Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth has faced tough questions from Republican and Democratic lawmakers about the Trump administration’s end game for the Iran war, the rising cost of the conflict and its impact on diminishing U.S. weapons stockpiles.

video:video.video:title: Philippine Senator at hearing after summons as part of probe into alleged extrajudicial killings
video:video.video:description: The International Criminal Court has unsealed an arrest warrant for a prominent Philippine senator linked to the deadly “war on drugs” overseen by ex-president Rodrigo Duterte, which allegedly involved the extrajudicial killings of suspects.

video:video.video:title: Israeli bulldozers demolish Palestinian shops on edge of town southeast of Jerusalem
video:video.video:description: Israeli bulldozers tore into Palestinian shops on the edge of a town southeast of Jerusalem, clearing land ahead of a settlement-linked road project.

video:video.video:title: Stars hit the red carpet for Cannes opening
video:video.video:description: The Cannes Film Festival kicked off Tuesday, with stars on the red carpet including Demi Moore, Stellan Skarsgård, Jane Fonda and Tyrese Gibson, along with director Peter Jackson, who was given an honorary Palme d’Or at the opening ceremony.

video:video.video:title: Tiger Woods’ prescription drug records will be handed over to prosecutors in Florida DUI case
video:video.video:description: Prosecutors agreed to the defense's request for a protective order limiting the release of records only to prosecutors, law enforcement officers, state experts and Woods' defense team.

video:video.video:title: Fire crew work to contain massive house fire in New Jersey neighborhood
video:video.video:description: Firefighters responded to a huge house fire in a New Jersey neighborhood on Tuesday afternoon, as crews attempted to extinguish the flames.

video:video.video:title: The humanitarian situation in Colombia has reached its worst level in a decade, says the International Committee of the Red Cross
video:video.video:description: The impact of armed conflict on civilians in Colombia over the past year has been the worst in a decade as the country's security situation deteriorates, the International Committee of the Red Cross said Tuesday in an annual report.

video:video.video:title: French hantavirus patient has most severe cardiopulmonary form, doctor says
video:video.video:description: A French woman infected with hantavirus after being repatriated from the MV Hondius cruise ship has the "most severe form of cardiopulmonary presentation," a doctor at the Paris hospital treating her said Tuesday.

video:video.video:title: WHO chief says all passengers onboard cruise ship hit by hantavirus outbreak disembarked
video:video.video:description: The WHO said it has confirmed 11 cases, including three people from the cruise who died. All confirmed cases are among passengers or crew of the MV Hondius cruise ship.

video:video.video:title: Taiwanese wary ahead of Trump-Xi summit amid concerns over island’s future
video:video.video:description: As U.S. President Donald Trump prepares for a high-stakes summit with Chinese leader Xi Jinping this week, many in Taiwan are watching nervously, uncertain whether the island’s future could become part of negotiations between Washington and Beijing.

video:video.video:title: Price shocks from Iran war power solar sales in energy-hungry Philippines
video:video.video:description: Panicked consumers in hard-hit Asia are turning to rooftop solar power as costs climb due to the Iran war.

video:video.video:title: A rare sanctuary in Congo looks after baby bonobos away from poaching threat
video:video.video:description: A sanctuary on the outskirts of Congo's capital of Kinshasa is the world’s only sanctuary for orphaned bonobos, usually rescued from poachers.

video:video.video:title: Some Japanese snack packages are turning black-and-white as Iran war depletes ink supply
video:video.video:description: The packaging on some snacks in Japan is turning a somber black-and-white, as the war in Iran disrupts the supply of an ingredient used in colored ink.

video:video.video:title: Quarantined cruise passenger gives Nebraska room tour, denies hantavirus infection
video:video.video:description: Jake Rosmarin of Boston is one of U.S. 15 passengers currently under observation in the National Quarantine Unit at the University of Nebraska Medical Center in Omaha after a hantavirus outbreak aboard a cruise ship.

video:video.video:title: Trump set to meet with Xi in Beijing as war and inflation weigh on his presidency
video:video.video:description: U.S. President Donald Trump is set to arrive in Beijing on Wednesday for his highly anticipated summit with Chinese leader Xi Jinping at a restless moment for a world worried about war, trade and artificial intelligence.

video:video.video:title: Man with assault rifle wildly shoots at drivers near Boston, wounding 2 before being shot
video:video.video:description: Video captured a man with an assault-style rifle fired randomly at passing cars while striding down a busy road outside Boston until he was wounded when a state trooper and a former Marine opened fire.

video:video.video:title: Trump set to arrive in Beijing as war and inflation weigh on his presidency
video:video.video:description: U.S. President Donald Trump is set to arrive in Beijing Wednesday for his highly anticipated summit with leader Xi Jinping at a restless moment for a world worried about war, trade and artificial intelligence.

video:video.video:title: South Korean startup puts cameras on workers to teach AI robots
video:video.video:description: RLWRLD is among a wave of South Korean high-tech firms and manufacturers competing in the unproven yet fiercely contested global market for “physical AI.”

video:video.video:title: Bomb rigged to rickshaw explodes in northwest Pakistan bazaar, killing at least 9 people
video:video.video:description: The attack took place in Lakki Marwat, a district in Khyber Pakhtunkhwa province, local police chief Azmat Ullah said.

video:video.video:title: Fire crew work to contain massive house fire in New Jersey neighborhood
video:video.video:description: Firefighters worked to contain a massive house fire in Norwood, New Jersey, on Tuesday afternoon. Aerial footage from WABC-TV shows the fire crew dousing the flames with water as large white and black plumes of smoke rise into the sky

video:video.video:title: Air Force trainer jet crashes, pilots eject safely
video:video.video:description: A U.S. Air Force training jet crashed Tuesday in west Alabama with both pilots ejecting safely, military officials said.

video:video.video:title: Tehran residents react to Iran-U.S impasse
video:video.video:description: Residents in Tehran reacted on Tuesday to comments by U.S President Donald Trump about the ceasefire between Iran and the U.S. (AP video by: Saeed Sarmadi)

video:video.video:title: How Austria's Cosmó is bringing star power to Eurovision
video:video.video:description: Cosmó, Austria's Eurovision Song Contest entrant, reveals whether fans recognize him without his blue star.

video:video.video:title: Austria’s Cosmó says Eurovision is going well
video:video.video:description: Cosmó, who represents host country Austria in this year's Eurovision Song Contest, says that he's heard great things from other participants and everyone is trying to focus on the music.

video:video.video:title: Gunshots heard in the Philippine Senate as authorities tried to arrest a senator
video:video.video:description: A burst of gunfire rang out Wednesday night in the Philippine Senate, where authorities have tried to arrest a senator, who is wanted by the International Criminal Court for a charge of crime against humanity, an Associated Press journalist and other witnesses said.

video:video.video:title: Torrential rains trigger severe flooding in district near Turkey’s Black Sea coast
video:video.video:description: The heavy rainfall late on Tuesday caused rivers in the Havza district of Samsun province to overflow, sending waters rushing down streets, carrying vehicles and debris.

video:video.video:title: Iran war drives solar sales in energy-hungry Asia
video:video.video:description: Soaring costs for fuel due to the Iran war are leading panicked consumers in hard-hit Asia toward rooftop solar power, a likely windfall for China as the world’s largest provider of solar technology.

video:video.video:title: Mýa returns with her first album in 8 years, the throwback 'Retrospect'
video:video.video:description: R&B singer Mýa is releasing "Retrospect," her tenth studio album and first full-length in eight years.

video:video.video:title: Procession, pomp, and ceremony: King Charles III lays out UK government agenda
video:video.video:description: In a speech to lawmakers laced with all the pomp that accompanies the ceremonial opening of Parliament, the king said the U.K.'

video:video.video:title: Brazil's beloved instant payment system faces scrutiny from the Trump administration
video:video.video:description: Brazil is a politically divided country, but there's one thing that those on all sides of the political spectrum love: PIX, the country's instant payment system that allows users to pay for everything, from ice cream on the beach to clothes in a shopping mall and even a car.

video:video.video:title: Israeli drone strikes hit highway south of Beirut, killing at least 8 people
video:video.video:description: Israeli drones struck three vehicles on the main highway south of Beirut Wednesday, killing eight people, including a woman and her two children, the Lebanese Health Ministry said.

video:video.video:title: Giant drills set to bore new train tunnels to New York City
video:video.video:description: The giant drills that will bore tunnels under the Hudson River for trains traveling to NYC are being assembled across the river in New Jersey. (AP Video: Ted Shaffrey)

video:video.video:title: Drug counselor who delivered ketamine to Matthew Perry gets 2 years
video:video.video:description: Erik Fleming, the licensed drug addiction counselor who delivered “Friends” star Matthew Perry the doses of ketamine that killed him, has been sentenced to two years in prison.

video:video.video:title: Beloved former mayor in Kansas fears he might be deported over voting controversy
video:video.video:description: While seeking citizenship in 2025, Joe Ceballos, 55, who was brought to the U.S. from Mexico by family when he was 4, and is a legal permanent resident, admitted during an interview that he had voted, apparently not knowing his status does not allow it, his lawyer said.

video:video.video:title: Alex Murdaugh's double-murder convictions are overturned
video:video.video:description: The South Carolina Supreme Court on Wednesday overturned the murder convictions and life sentence of disgraced lawyer Alex Murdaugh in the shooting deaths of his wife and younger son.

video:video.video:title: Come with me to a FIFA World Cup preview
video:video.video:description: Los Angeles is a host city of the FIFA World Cup, and AP got a preview of the food, transportation and stadium set up. Follow Sarah Jones-Smith inside.

video:video.video:title: Historic Maryland racetrack will host its final races, including Preakness, before shutting down
video:video.video:description: Once home to packed grandstands and stars like Secretariat, Laurel now draws only a few hundred regulars on many race days.

video:video.video:title: Ukrainian drone pilots train NATO troops
video:video.video:description: Ukrainian drone pilots were invited to train NATO forces in Sweden - and delivered a critical message to the alliance.

video:video.video:title: Hockey drama 'Off Campus' uses 'Bridgerton' template
video:video.video:description: New hockey romance series "Off Campus" has a model like "Bridgerton," where each season will focus on a different relationship. (May 13)

video:video.video:title: Gunfire breaks out in Philippine Senate where authorities tried to arrest a senator
video:video.video:description: A burst of gunfire rang out Wednesday night in the Philippine Senate, where authorities have tried to arrest a senator who is wanted by the International Criminal Court for a charge of crime against humanity, an Associated Press journalist and other witnesses said.

video:video.video:title: Ukrainian drone pilots turn military exercise in Sweden into critical warning for NATO
video:video.video:description: Ukrainian drone pilots turned a military exercise in Sweden into a critical warning for NATO. The pilots, playing the role of the enemy in the exercise, said they destroyed Swedish forces with drones — and that if the exercise had been a real life scenario, the Western forces would have been dead.

video:video.video:title: Trump arrives in Beijing to meet Xi as war and inflation weigh on his presidency
video:video.video:description: US President Donald Trump arrived in Beijing on Wednesday for his highly anticipated summit with Chinese leader Xi Jinping at a restless moment for a world worried about war, trade and artificial intelligence.

video:video.video:title: Doc featuring Sandra Oh explores Asian American, Pacific Islander identities
video:video.video:description: Timed for release during AAPI Heritage Month, documentary film “The A List: 15 Stories from Asian and Pacific Diasporas” has dropped on HBO Max. (May 13)

video:video.video:title: FBI Director Kash Patel denies drinking allegations in heated Senate exchange
video:video.video:description: FBI Director Kash Patel angrily lashed out at a Democratic lawmaker during a budget hearing Tuesday, calling allegations that he drinks excessively on the job and has been unreachable at times to his staff “unequivocally, categorically false.”

video:video.video:title: 'Off Campus' provides a new, steamy, hockey romance
video:video.video:description: Smash hit drama "Heated Rivalry" doesn't return until 2027 but Prime Video's new series "Off Campus," also about hunky hockey players, is now streaming. (May 13)

video:video.video:title: US grocery prices rose in April, but gas spikes weren't the only reason
video:video.video:description: Americans paid more for their groceries last month, but high gasoline prices due to the Iran war is only one of the reasons why.

video:video.video:title: AP top stories May 13
video:video.video:description: Here's the latest for Wednesday, May 13th: Trump in China to meet with Xi in Beijing; Prosecutors to retry Murdaugh after overturned murder convictions; Gunfire breaks out in the Philippine Senate; Tech improving the quality of life for dementia patients.

video:video.video:title: Princess Catherine visits Italy on first solo trip abroad after cancer goes into remission
video:video.video:description: Britain’s Princess Catherine made her first overseas trip since announcing her cancer was in remission, traveling to Italy on Wednesday for a two-day tour focused on an early childhood educational approach that was developed here and exported globally.

video:video.video:title: Kouri Richins, who wrote a children’s book on grief after her husband’s death, gets life sentence
video:video.video:description: Kouri Richins was convicted in March of aggravated murder for lacing her husband Eric Richins’ cocktail with five times the lethal dose of fentanyl at their home near Park City in 2022.

video:video.video:title: Prosecutors to retry Alex Murdaugh after murder convictions overturned
video:video.video:description: Alex Murdaugh’s murder convictions and life sentence for the deaths of his wife and son were overturned Wednesday by the South Carolina Supreme Court.

video:video.video:title: Drug counselor who delivered ketamine to Matthew Perry gets 2 years
video:video.video:description: Erik Fleming, the licensed drug addiction counselor who delivered “Friends” star Matthew Perry the doses of ketamine that killed him, has been sentenced to two years in prison.

video:video.video:title: Mýa releases first album in 8 years
video:video.video:description: Mya reveals which artists she "salutes" on "Retrospect," her first album in eight years.

video:video.video:title: 'Ocean Dream' blue-green diamond sells for more than $17 million at Christie's auction in Geneva
video:video.video:description: A 5.5-carat triangular-cut diamond billed as the largest fancy vivid blue-green diamond known to exist sold for more than 13.5 million Swiss francs ($17.3 million) on Wednesday, Christie’s said, calling it a record price for a stone of its kind sold at auction.

video:video.video:title: Pop-up arcade game in DC mocks Trump administration's handling of Iran war
video:video.video:description: The mini arcade is the latest piece from Secret Handshake, a resistance art group that has installed several pieces of artwork on the Mall to protest the Trump administration, including a Golden toilet.

video:video.video:title: King Charles III lays out UK government agenda as Starmer's job hangs in the balance
video:video.video:description: In a speech to lawmakers laced with all the pomp that accompanies the ceremonial opening of Parliament, the king said the U.K.'

video:video.video:title: South Carolina Attorney General says he will seek 'speedy' retrial in Alex Murdaugh murder case
video:video.video:description: Prosecutors say they plan to retry disgraced lawyer Alex Murdaugh after the South Carolina Supreme Court overturned his murder convictions and life sentence for the deaths of his wife and son because the court clerk at his trial suggested he was guilty.

video:video.video:title: Russia launches massive daytime drone attack on Ukraine, firing at least 800 drones
video:video.video:description: Russia launched a massive daytime drone attack on Ukraine on Wednesday, firing at least 800 drones across 20 regions of the country.

video:video.video:title: Nicolas Cage channels pop art, classic Hollywood in ‘Spider-Noir’
video:video.video:description: Nicolas Cage said he was inspired by pop artist Roy Lichtenstein and classic Hollywood actors as he developed “Spider-Noir.” (May 13)

video:video.video:title: 'Fast & Furious' cast reunite at Cannes Film Festival
video:video.video:description: Vin Diesel, Meadow Walker and the 'Fast & Furious' stars celebrated the 25th anniversary of the franchise with a special midnight screening. (May 13)

video:video.video:title: How 'Breaking Bad' helped Nicolas Cage see the possibilities in television
video:video.video:description: Nicolas Cage says working on “Spider-Noir” as a series gave him more creative freedom than film. (May 13)

video:video.video:title: Russia hits Kyiv with drones and ballistic missiles, injuring at least 4
video:video.video:description: Russia attacked Ukraine's capital with a mass drone and missile attack in the early morning of Thursday, injuring at least four people, local authorities said.

video:video.video:title: A Vienna cafe offers a welcome for Israel supporters amid tensions at the Eurovision Song Contest
video:video.video:description: Vienna's famed coffeehouses are embracing the Eurovision Song Contest. They have also been touched by tensions over Israel’s inclusion in the sequin-drenched pop music extravaganza.

video:video.video:title: Digital workers in Gaza try to earn badly needed incomes and find a form of escape from adversities
video:video.video:description: Digital freelancing became popular more than a decade ago in Gaza. But traditional sectors shrank after Hamas seized control of the strip in 2007, as Israel’s intensified blockade devastated agriculture, manufacturing and other industries.

video:video.video:title: Scientists develop an autonomous robot to map endangered coral reefs
video:video.video:description: Researchers in the United States have developed an autonomous underwater robot that can listen to coral reefs, track marine life and identify biodiversity hotspots.

video:video.video:title: War worsens Lebanon's economic crisis with job losses, price gouging and slow business
video:video.video:description: The U.S.-Israeli war with Iran, and the closure of the Strait of Hormuz, have sent economic shock waves across the Mideast.

video:video.video:title: California Governor candidate Xavier Becerra rallies crowd in campaign event
video:video.video:description: Candidate for California Governor Xavier Becerra held a campaign rally in San Francisco Wednesday night.

video:video.video:title: Invalides monument in Paris: 350 years of care for wounded soldiers and war victims
video:video.video:description: World-famous as the resting place of Napoleon, the gilded dome of the Invalides in Paris has drawn millions of visitors, but behind the landmark’s grand façade was a lesser-known mission: serving as a home and hospital for wounded soldiers and victims of war for more than 350 years.

video:video.video:title: Chinese President Xi welcomes visiting US President Trump in Beijing
video:video.video:description: Chinese President Xi Jinping hosts a welcoming ceremony for visiting US President Donald Trump ahead of a bilateral meeting.

video:video.video:title: Xi hosts Trump for a welcoming ceremony in Beijing
video:video.video:description: Chinese President Xi Jinping hosts a welcoming ceremony for visiting U.S. President Donald Trump ahead of a bilateral meeting.

video:video.video:title: Presidents Xi Jinping and Donald Trump sit down and begin bilateral talks
video:video.video:description: Presidents Xi Jinping and Donald Trump met on Thursday in Beijing for a U.S.-China summit where few breakthroughs are expected on divisive issues ranging from the Iran war, trade, technology and Taiwan.

video:video.video:title: 'China is beautiful,' Trump says during Temple of Heaven visit with Xi in Beijing
video:video.video:description: U.S. President Donald Trump arrived at the Temple of Heaven with Chinese President Xi Jinping after the pair discussed trade, Taiwan and other differences in the U.S.
